name: Habitat For Humanity of the Charlotte Region - Financial Literacy Center
description_short: Habitat for Humanity's Money Matters is a free financial literacy program offering workshops on budgeting, credit building, and homeownership for employees and community members.
description_long:
Habitat for Humanity of the Charlotte Region operates the Money Matters Financial Literacy Program, a non-profit educational initiative designed to strengthen financial wellness across the community. As part of Habitat's broader mission, Money Matters extends beyond traditional housing services to address the foundational financial knowledge gaps that prevent individuals from achieving economic stability and homeownership.

The program delivers three structured workshops covering critical financial topics including budgeting, saving strategies, credit building fundamentals, and pathways to homeownership. Participants gain practical knowledge and tools to improve financial stability through debt reduction, increased savings, and credit improvement. A distinctive feature is the program's incentive component for graduates, offering tangible rewards such as debt reduction assistance or closing cost help to support participants in achieving their specific financial goals.

Money Matters distinguishes itself by targeting both employee populations and broader community members, positioning financial literacy as a workplace benefit that enhances retention while simultaneously strengthening community economic resilience. The program emphasizes measurable outcomes: improving financial stability, boosting employee engagement, promoting homeownership, and contributing to economic growth. Rather than selling financial products, Habitat positions itself as a trusted community partner invested in genuine financial empowerment.

As a Habitat for Humanity program, Money Matters carries the credibility of an established non-profit with decades of community work. The primary limitation is geographic scope—the program appears specific to the Charlotte Region. Additionally, the website emphasizes organizational partnerships and cohort-based delivery, suggesting the program requires institutional sponsorship rather than serving walk-in individual consumers. Details about certification credentials, counselor qualifications, or specific workshop content are limited.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free financial literacy program with no product sales or hidden fees
Covers comprehensive topics including budgeting, saving, credit building, and homeownership
Provides tangible incentives for graduates such as debt reduction or closing cost assistance
Offered through established, trusted non-profit Habitat for Humanity
Designed for both employees and community members, broadening accessibility
Structured three-workshop format focused on practical financial skill-building
Emphasizes homeownership as measurable economic outcome, not just financial awareness
cons: Geographic limitation to Charlotte Region only
Appears to require institutional/organizational sponsorship rather than individual enrollment
Limited information about counselor qualifications or NFCC certification status on website
Website lacks specific details about workshop schedules, duration, or access procedures
No indication of one-on-one counseling availability separate from cohort workshops
best_for: Employees seeking financial wellness support through their employer
Charlotte-area community members interested in homeownership preparation
Organizations wanting to implement employee financial wellness programs
Individuals focused on credit building and debt reduction with accountability incentives
diagnosis: Money Matters is best for Charlotte-area employees or community members seeking free, comprehensive financial education through an established non-profit, particularly those interested in homeownership. The main caveat is that the program appears structured around organizational partnerships and cohort delivery rather than drop-in individual counseling, and specific enrollment procedures are not detailed on the website.
services: Graduate incentive programs for debt reduction
Closing cost assistance for qualified participants
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
